cutting_teeth.name: Cutting Teeth
cutting_teeth.description:
The first order of business will be to put a little strength in your backbone. I could send you out to the Barrens to hunt kodo, but well, in all honesty, you're more useful to us alive than dead.
I believe you would find a good match with the mottled boars you'll find to the north of here.

sting_of_the_scorpid.name: Sting of the Scorpid
sting_of_the_scorpid.description: Collect 10 scorpid tails from the workers that infest the Valley of Trials.

sarkoth.name: Sarkoth
sarkoth.description: Sarkoth, a great scorpid, lurks deep within the Valley of Trials. Slay five of his workers to draw him out, then claim his claw as proof of your victory.

vile_familiars.name: Vile Familiars
vile_familiars.description: The Burning Blade Clan has bound demonic imps to haunt the Valley of Trials. Slay twelve of these vile familiars and thin their numbers.

burning_blade_medallion.name: Burning Blade Medallion
burning_blade_medallion.description: Yarrog Baneshadow, a warlock of the Burning Blade Clan, commands the imps in the valley. Slay five of his imps to provoke him, then defeat Yarrog and take his medallion.

break_a_few_eggs.name: Break a Few Eggs
break_a_few_eggs.description:
The bloodtalon taillashers of the Echo Isles lay their eggs amongst the rocks on the islands. Those eggs are good for a breakfast stew!

Could you bring me a few eggs? The taillashers can be fierce, and protect their eggs to the death more often than not. I'll understand if it proves too hard... but those eggs sure would taste mighty fine.

practical_prey.name: Practical Prey
practical_prey.description:
The tigers that stalk through the Echo Isles are a menace to the young trolls that train there, and their pelts make for fine armor and warm blankets.

There has been a rise in the population of the beasts, and our needs are starting to overwhelm our stocks. Hunt the tigers of the Echo Isles and return with their furs.

a_solvent_spirit.name: A Solvent Spirit
a_solvent_spirit.description:
I have need of a very specific substance -- a solvent spirit, distilled from equal parts transparency and luminescence.

To make such a spirit, I need intact makrura eyes, and vials of crawler mucus. You can find them on any makrura or crawler in Durotar.

Four of the eyes, and eight vials of the mucus. Bring them to me.

zalazane.name: Zalazane
zalazane.description:
Zalazane has taken the Echo Isles from us -- our home, the home of the Darkspear! From there he sends his trolls to the mainland, to hex our people and drive them insane.

I know this is a hard thing I ask, but you must go to the Echo Isles and kill the voodoo trolls and hexed trolls that follow Zalazane. There is no saving them. And when you have done this, face Zalazane himself and bring me his head.

Then the Echo Isles will be ours once more.

minshinaas_skull.name: Minshina's Skull
minshinaas_skull.description:
My heart goes out to those poor souls who have lost their minds to Zalazane's hex. There was one -- my apprentice -- Minshina, a young troll of tremendous promise. He tried to resist Zalazane's will and in the end Zalazane murdered him.

His spirit remains trapped within his skull which Zalazane keeps as some kind of trophy, in a circle of power he erected on the Echo Isles. Voodoo slaves guard that circle day and night.

If you can cut through his guardians, find the skull and bring it to me, I will try to free his spirit.

objectives.description: Defeat the Voodoo Slave guarding the Circle of Skulls

skull_rock.name: Skull Rock
skull_rock.description:
The Burning Blade has infested the cave east of Orgrimmar known as Skull Rock. Inside that cave they perform vile rituals, and burn their own flesh with Searing Collars -- iron bands they believe attune them to demonic power.

Bring me six of those collars. With them I may be able to divine more about the cult's true purpose.

felweaver_scornn.name: Felweaver Scornn
felweaver_scornn.description:
You have weakened the Burning Blade's grip on Skull Rock -- but their most dangerous practitioner, Felweaver Scornn, still holds the deepest chamber. While he lives, the cult will not break.

Fight through his disciples and drive him into the open. Return with proof of his death, and Skull Rock can finally be cleansed.

dark_storms.name: Dark Storms
dark_storms.description:
We cannot allow the Burning Blade a foothold in Durotar! We must destroy them before their evil festers!

In Thunder Ridge, to the northwest of Razor Hill, the goblin warlock Fizzle Darkstorm and his cultist minions lurk. They practice dark rituals that twist the storms above the ridge.

Kill his followers until he emerges, then slay him and bring me his claw as proof.

vanquish_the_betrayers.name: Vanquish the Betrayers
vanquish_the_betrayers.description:
The Kul Tiras navy has seized Tiragarde Keep -- a clear violation of any agreement between their people and ours. Admiral Proudmoore's sailors and marines infest that keep, and while they hold it we cannot call Durotar safe.

Go there. Kill ten of their sailors and eight of their marines. Their Watch Commander, Zalaphil, holds the garrison together -- cut him down and the occupation will fall apart.

the_admirals_orders.name: The Admiral's Orders
the_admirals_orders.description: Gar'Thok speaks: We know Proudmoore planned this occupation in advance -- the question is why and what comes next. Search the papers and documents carried by the Kul Tiras forces at Tiragarde Keep. If you find official orders from the Admiral himself, bring them to me.

objectives.description: Find the Admiral's Orders in Tiragarde Keep

carry_your_weight.name: Carry Your Weight
carry_your_weight.description:
I used to fight alongside Thrall himself -- now I watch from a tower and craft what I can for the fighters below. What I need is canvas. The humans at Tiragarde Keep carry canvas packs and tents; the centaur use it for their supply wrappings too.

Bring me eight canvas scraps and I'll put them to good use.

winds_in_the_desert.name: Winds in the Desert
winds_in_the_desert.description:
Harpies! The last caravan shipment was stolen by those feathered thieves in Razorwind Canyon. Follow the big canyon to the south -- you'll find the ravine cut right into the west and east sides.

Get back my five sacks of supplies. I don't care what you have to do.

securing_the_lines.name: Securing the Lines
securing_the_lines.description:
Harpies won't stop bothering us and the caravans won't stop being attacked unless we do something about it, eh? The Dustwind Savages and Storm Witches infest Razorwind Canyon -- they're the ones organizing the raids.

If we kill enough of them there, the routes will be safe.

encroachment.name: Encroachment
encroachment.description:
The Razormane quilboar push farther and farther from their lands into Durotar. They send Quilboar, Scouts, Dustrunners, and Battleguards -- each type a different arm of their assault.

Kill them all. Make sure they understand there are consequences for their actions.

geolord_mottle.name: Geolord Mottle
geolord_mottle.description:
The Razormane quilboar are organized around their shamans and warleaders. The most dangerous of these is Geolord Mottle -- a quilboar earth-shaman who commands their Battleguards from within the quilboar grounds.

Cut through his Battleguard escort and put him down. Bring back his totem as proof.

thwarting_kolkar_aggression.name: Thwarting Kolkar Aggression
thwarting_kolkar_aggression.description:
The Kolkar centaur have laid plans to assault Horde caravans passing through the Barrens. Their strategy is written in attack plans scattered throughout Kolkar Crag.

Retrieve three of those plans. We need to understand the scope of their operation if we're to stop it.

warlord_kolkanis.name: Warlord Kolkanis
warlord_kolkanis.description:
The Kolkar centaur who attack our caravans answer to one warlord: Kolkanis. He drives his Outrunners in waves against Horde supply lines.

Fight through his Outrunner escort and take him down. Bring back his battle standard -- the centaur rally to it, and without it their raids will falter.

centaur_bracers.name: Centaur Bracers
centaur_bracers.description: The Kolkar centaur who haunt the Barrens are bold enough to attack the Crossroads itself. Collect fifteen iron bracers from their slain warriors. Let the sight of them fill our people with confidence that the centaur can be defeated.

the_searing_blade.name: The Searing Blade
the_searing_blade.description: Deep beneath Orgrimmar, the Searing Blade cult festers within Ragefire Chasm. They plot to overthrow the Warchief and throw open the gates to demonic power. Descend into the chasm and kill twelve of their cultists before their plot can be carried out.

rite_of_strength.name: Rite of Strength
rite_of_strength.description:
I have watched you grow from a small calf into a brave young hunter. Now it is time to prove yourself worthy to wear the title of warrior.

East of camp lies Brambleblade Ravine. The Bristleback quilboar live there, and they raid our village and threaten our hunters. Go there and collect twelve of their belts. Bring them back as proof of your valor.

the_hunt_continues.name: The Hunt Continues
the_hunt_continues.description:
A hunter must understand that the beasts of this land provide more than just food and materials. They test our skill and our patience.

The mountain cougars prowl the hills south of Camp Narache. Bring me ten of their pelts. The elders will see that you are worthy of further training.

poison_water.name: Poison Water
poison_water.description:
The Venture Company and their servants have tainted our sacred water wells. To craft a cleansing totem, I need components from the creatures of the land.

To the southwest, prairie wolves run in packs. To the west, plainstriders wander the open grass. Bring me six prairie wolf paws and four plainstrider talons.

thunderhorn_totem.name: Thunderhorn Totem
thunderhorn_totem.description:
The goblins' poison seeps into the Thunderhorn Water Well. To cleanse it I must craft a special totem, and for that I need claws from the great cats of the plain.

Hunt the prairie stalkers for their stalker claws, and the flatland cougars for their cougar claws. Bring me six of each.

wildmane_totem.name: Wildmane Totem
wildmane_totem.description:
One more well remains tainted — the Wildmane Water Well. For this cleansing I need the teeth of a fierce predator: the prairie wolf alpha. The alphas wander near Thunder Bluff itself.

Bring me eight of their teeth.

swoop_hunting.name: Swoop Hunting
swoop_hunting.description:
The swoops may be found anywhere in Mulgore, but your eyes must be sharp to spot them and your hooves must be quick to catch them. They are elusive creatures, always circling above waiting for prey to fall.

Bring me eight trophy swoop quills. Each one proves you have brought down one of these cunning birds.

mazzranache_quest.name: Mazzranache
mazzranache_quest.description:
The creature named Mazzranache bit me while I was traveling -- now I burn with infection. My grandmother used to make a remedy for bites like this, but she needed hard-to-find animal parts.

I need a prairie wolf heart, a flatland cougar femur, a plainstrider scale, and a swoop gizzard. All four together, or the remedy won't work.

sharing_the_land.name: Sharing the Land
sharing_the_land.description:
The Palemane gnolls refuse any attempt to communicate with them and slay the wildlife of Mulgore unbridled. They camp to the south and west, killing our kodo calves and stripping hides from the beasts of the land.

We cannot share the land with creatures who show no respect for it. Kill ten of their tanners, eight of their skinners, and five of their poachers.

the_venture_co.name: The Venture Co.
the_venture_co.description:
The Venture Company has refused to honor the Tauren's refusal of mining rights. They dig our land without permission, claiming Mulgore's minerals for themselves.

Go to their mine northeast of the ravaged caravan and send them a message. Kill fourteen of their workers and six of their supervisors.

supervisor_fizsprocket.name: Supervisor Fizsprocket
supervisor_fizsprocket.description:
The Venture Company's workers are only half the problem. Their site director, Supervisor Fizsprocket, is the one who orchestrated the entire operation to drive the tauren from our lands.

Find him in the mine east of the ravaged caravan. Cut through his workers to draw him out, then put him down and bring me his clipboard.

preparation_for_ceremony.name: Preparation for Ceremony
preparation_for_ceremony.description:
My brother's ceremony is soon and I have not yet completed his headdress. I need feathers -- azure and bronze -- from the Windfury harpies that roost to the north and northwest.

Bring me six azure feathers and six bronze feathers. Time is short.

dwarven_digging.name: Dwarven Digging
dwarven_digging.description:
The dwarves of Bael'dun have been disturbing the land and stampeding the kodo. Their picks are what drive the excavation.

Collect five of their prospector's picks and bring them to me. Without their tools, the dig will grind to a halt.

rites_of_the_earthmother.name: Rites of the Earthmother
rites_of_the_earthmother.description:
Your final rite is to prove your hunting mastery against a truly great beast. Arra'chea -- an elder kodo who has roamed Mulgore since before the Tauren settled here -- grazes near the great plains below Thunder Bluff.

Track her down. Drive away the other kodo that protect her, then face her yourself. Return with her horn and Cairne Bloodhoof will know you are ready to walk among warriors.

plainstrider_menace.name: Plainstrider Menace
plainstrider_menace.description:
The Plains Striders are becoming a menace on the Crossroads road. Their flocks trample supply caravans and attack lone travelers without provocation.

You must thin their numbers. Kill fifteen Plains Striders south of the Crossroads. Do this and I'll know you're ready for harder hunts.

the_zhevra.name: The Zhevra
the_zhevra.description:
The zhevra is a creature of the Barrens, and it's one you should know well. They run in small herds to the south and east of the Crossroads. The zhevra's hoof is a unique four-toed structure unlike any other beast on Kalimdor.

Bring me four zhevra hooves and I'll teach you something useful about Barrens hunting.

raptor_thieves.name: Raptor Thieves
raptor_thieves.description:
The Sunscale raptors have been raiding our supply wagons. They're smart enough to work in packs and fast enough to outrun any merchant's horse.

There's only one answer: kill them. Take down twelve Sunscale Lashtails and bring back eight of their heads to prove it's done.

lost_in_battle.name: Lost in Battle
lost_in_battle.description:
A column of Alliance soldiers was ambushed by Kolkar centaur on the road west of the Crossroads. None survived. The centaur made off with their weapons and equipment.

Those were good fighters who deserved better. Find five bundles of the fallen warriors' arms and bring them back so we can give them a proper burial.

a_new_threat.name: A New Threat
a_new_threat.description:
The Bristleback quilboar have fortified Thorn Hill to the east of the Crossroads. Their geomancers are raising earthen walls and their warriors have started raiding the roads openly.

This threat must be eliminated before they grow bolder. Kill fifteen Bristleback Quilboar and eight of their Geomancers.

harpy_raiders.name: Harpy Raiders
harpy_raiders.description:
The Witchwing harpies nest around the Stagnant Oasis and raid caravans traveling the northern road. Their talons are as sharp as any blade and their lightning-calls can stop a warrior cold.

Go to the Stagnant Oasis and kill ten of their warriors and five of their Slayers. Bring back their talons as proof.

the_disruption_ends.name: The Disruption Ends
the_disruption_ends.description:
The Kolkar centaur have grown increasingly bold. Their raiders hit the Crossroads road daily and their marauders now threaten the road to Ratchet.

Ride to Lushwater Oasis and strike the heart of their operation. Kill ten Kolkar Pack Runners and eight Kolkar Marauders. Collect eight of their war horns to show that the disruption is finished.

hezrul_bloodmark_quest.name: Hezrul Bloodmark
hezrul_bloodmark_quest.description:
Hezrul Bloodmark is the chieftain of the Kolkar clan at Lushwater Oasis. Every raid, every ambushed caravan, every fallen traveler on the Crossroads road � it all flows from his orders.

Kill his guards and draw him out. Bring me his blood as proof. Only then will the centaur threat be truly broken.

deviate_hides.name: Deviate Hides
deviate_hides.description:
The creatures that inhabit the Wailing Caverns are not normal beasts -- they have been transformed by energy seeping from below. Their hides are unlike anything a leatherworker encounters on the surface.

Bring me twenty deviate hides from the creatures inside the Wailing Caverns.

leaders_of_the_fang.name: Leaders of the Fang
leaders_of_the_fang.description:
The Druids of the Fang were once members of the Cenarion Circle who came to cleanse the Wailing Caverns of corruption. They failed -- and were themselves corrupted.

Their leader, Lord Serpentis, now commands the entire ecosystem inside those caverns. Cut through his disciples and bring down Serpentis himself. Collect his fang as proof of your victory.

objectives.description: Collect the Fang of the Fang

the_balance_of_nature.name: The Balance of Nature
the_balance_of_nature.description:
You've come to Aldrassil at an important time. Something is wrong in Shadowglen — imps called Grellkin have crept into our grove, and they disturb the natural balance our druids have spent years cultivating.

Kill seven of the Grellkin lurking in the lower grove and bring me their fangs as proof. Together we will restore what has been upset.

webwood_venom.name: Webwood Venom
webwood_venom.description:
The Webwood spiders have claimed far too much of the lower grove. The venom they inject paralyzes prey for hours, and I fear what happens if they grow bold enough to attack the young initiates.

Collect seven venom sacs from the Webwood Lurkers in Shadowglen. I'll use them to develop an antidote.

webwood_egg.name: Webwood Egg
webwood_egg.description:
Rumors have come to Aldrassil of a spider nest deeper in the grove. I need to understand how they reproduce so we can control their numbers.

Bring me ten Webwood eggs from the lurkers and spiders in Shadowglen. If we can stop them from hatching, we stop the infestation.

bloodfeather_territory.name: Bloodfeather Territory
bloodfeather_territory.description:
The Bloodfeather harpies have claimed Oracle Glade as their own and struck down two of our scouts this season. Their Wind Witches call down lightning on anything that moves through the clearing.

We must push them back. Kill ten Bloodfeather Harpies and five of their Wind Witches, then bring me eight of their feathers as proof of the clanflock's defeat.

seek_redemption.name: Seek Redemption!
seek_redemption.description:
Something is wrong with the treants of Teldrassil. The Timberlings, once gentle spirits who tended the roots of our great tree, have become hostile and mindless, attacking any who come near.

The corruption must be answered. Kill twelve Timberlings and five of the larger Timberling Mires you'll find deeper in the grove. Perhaps then the druids can begin to understand what has turned them.

timberling_seeds.name: Timberling Seeds
timberling_seeds.description:
I have been studying the Timberlings for months, and I believe the corruption in their seeds holds the key to understanding the blight spreading through Teldrassil's roots.

Bring me eight Timberling Seeds. I've noticed the corrupted ones carry seeds at a later stage of corruption than the saplings. Be careful — they're much less predictable than they look.

gnarlpine_corruption.name: Gnarlpine Corruption
gnarlpine_corruption.description:
Starbreeze Village was once our peaceful home. Now the Gnarlpine furbolg, corrupted by some dark force, have driven us out and claim the village for themselves.

We cannot let this stand. Go to Gnarlpine Hold and cut down ten of their Ambushers and eight of their Hunters. Show them that the night elves will not surrender Teldrassil.

reclaiming_starbreeze.name: Reclaiming Starbreeze Village
reclaiming_starbreeze.description:
The Gnarlpine shamans and mystics are the source of the corruption flowing through our village. They perform dark rituals that twist more furbolg to their cause with each passing night.

Raid their gathering grounds and put down eight Gnarlpine Mystics and six of their Shamans. Recover six of their bone totems as proof the rituals have been stopped.

the_sleeping_druid.name: The Sleeping Druid
the_sleeping_druid.description:
A druid named Oben Rageclaw entered Ban'ethil Barrow Den to sleep in the Emerald Dream and has not awakened. We believe the Gnarlpine Defenders and Ursa who have invaded the den are preventing his spirit from returning.

Fight your way through the Gnarlpine warriors inside and find Oben. Cut down eight Defenders and six of the hulking Ursa who block the inner passages.

ursal_the_mauler_quest.name: Ursal the Mauler
ursal_the_mauler_quest.description:
Ursal the Mauler leads the Gnarlpine deep inside Ban'ethil Barrow Den. As long as he lives, the furbolg corruption in Teldrassil will only grow stronger — he is their champion and their source of will.

Fight through his Ursa guard and bring Ursal down. Return to me with his paw as proof of your victory.

q_first_blood.name: First Blood
q_first_blood.description: Prove your worth by defeating enemies in the Valley of Trials.

q_boar_hunt.name: Boar Hunt
q_boar_hunt.description: The boars of Durotar have been disrupting supply routes.

q_fel_threat.name: Fel Threat
q_fel_threat.description: Imps and Felstalkers have been spotted near the Valley of Trials.

q_echo_isles_defense.name: Defending the Isles
q_echo_isles_defense.description: Clear dangerous creatures from the Echo Isles.

objectives.description: Defeat 6 creatures on the Echo Isles

q_kolkar_threat.name: Kolkar Aggression
q_kolkar_threat.description: Show the Kolkar centaur the cost of defiance.

replenishing_healing_crystals.name: Replenishing the Healing Crystals
replenishing_healing_crystals.description:
Many of our people still suffer from injuries sustained when the Exodar crashed. Our healers work tirelessly, but our supply of healing crystals is dangerously low.

The manaforged fragments wandering the crash site shed these crystals when slain. Gather eight of them for me so that we may tend to our wounded.

volatile_mutations.name: Volatile Mutations
volatile_mutations.description:
The mana energies released by the Exodar's crash have twisted many of the local creatures into mindless, violent beasts. The volatile mutants attack our survivors on sight, and the infected nightstalkers prowl the edges of our camp.

I need you to put down ten of the volatile mutants and eight infected nightstalkers before they overrun our position.

crazed_owlkin_quest.name: The Crazed Owlkin
crazed_owlkin_quest.description:
The owlkin of Ammen Vale were once peaceful creatures content to watch over the forests. The mana surge from the Exodar's crash drove them to a state of permanent frenzy — they attack our people without provocation.

We cannot allow survivors to be picked off by these crazed beasts. Slay twelve of the crazed owlkin and return to me.

moongraze_hunt.name: A Favorite Treat
moongraze_hunt.description:
The moongraze stags and bucks that roam this island provide meat for our people and materials for our craftsmen. However, since the crash our hunters have been occupied with defense, and our stores are empty.

Hunt the moongraze in the hills beyond Azure Watch and collect ten sets of their tendons. We'll use them to feed the wounded and to craft the tools our people need.

ravager_threat.name: The Ravager Threat
ravager_threat.description:
The ravagers from Draenor were transported to Azuremyst Isle when the Exodar crashed. They have multiplied quickly and now roam our hills in packs, threatening the new settlements.

Kill twelve ravagers and recover eight of their hides. Our leatherworkers can use them, and reducing the pack size will make the roads safer.

naga_at_the_docks.name: Naga at the Docks
naga_at_the_docks.description:
The Wrathscale naga have seized the coastal docks north of Azure Watch, cutting off our access to the sea. Their naiads and warriors have killed three of our scouts already.

Drive them back. Slay ten Wrathscale Naiads and six Wrathscale Naga and return to me. We must show them that Azuremyst belongs to the Draenei.

wrathscale_evidence.name: The Wrathscale's Ambition
wrathscale_evidence.description:
We know the Wrathscale naga have claimed the docks, but we don't yet understand what they want here. Their bracelets bear markings I've seen before — symbols indicating the personal seal of a naga sea witch.

Recover five of their bracelets from the Wrathscale Naiads at the docks. The inscriptions may tell us who sent this clan to Azuremyst and what they seek.

bristlelimb_troubles.name: The Bristlelimb Tribe
bristlelimb_troubles.description:
The Stillpine furbolg tell us that the Bristlelimb tribe was their kin — peaceful creatures who tended the inland forests. But demonic taint has driven them to madness, and they now raid Stillpine Hold and attack our people.

Fight your way through Stillpine Hold's outskirts. Kill ten Bristlelimb Furbolg and six of their Shamans, then recover five of their totems. The Stillpine elders will use them to attempt a purification ritual.

the_kurken_quest.name: The Kurken is Angry!
the_kurken_quest.description:
The Kurken — the Stillpine furbolg's sacred guardian beast — has been twisted by the same demonic corruption as the Bristlelimb tribe. It rampages through Stillpine Hold, killing Stillpine and Draenei alike.

Fight through the corrupted Bristlelimb to reach the Kurken, and put it down. Bring back its idol as proof of the beast's end so that High Chief Stillpine can at last perform the rites of mourning.

sironas_quest.name: Sironas Must Be Stopped!
sironas_quest.description:
The man'ari eredar Sironas commands the blood elves who have taken Silvermyst Isle. She was dispatched by our ancient enemies to destroy the Draenei while we are weak from the crash.

Fight through her blood elf agents on Silvermyst Isle. Kill eight Blood Elf Trackers and collect five of their insignias as evidence of their orders. Then confront and destroy Sironas herself and bring back her signet ring — proof that this threat to our people has been ended.

wrath_of_the_hounds.name: Wrath of the Hounds
wrath_of_the_hounds.description:
The wrath hounds roaming Bloodmyst Isle are not wild beasts — the iron collars around their necks bear Sunhawk markings. The blood elves bred them as forward scouts to flush out Draenei survivors.

Kill twelve wrath hounds and recover eight of their collars as evidence of the Sunhawk's involvement. I want Prophet Velen to understand exactly what we're dealing with.

corrupted_flight.name: Corrupted Flight
corrupted_flight.description:
The corrupted owlkin and ravagers of Bloodmyst Isle reproduce faster than we can track. The corruption has removed whatever instinct kept them from multiplying unchecked, and they are overrunning the eastern paths.

Cull ten corrupted owlkin and eight bloodmyst ravagers. Gather five ravager fangs as well — our leatherworkers can use them to craft tools now that our stores from Argus are exhausted.

sunhawk_spies.name: The Sunhawk Spies
sunhawk_spies.description:
We have spotted blood elf spies posing as travelers in the hills north of Blood Watch. These Sunhawk agents are mapping our patrol routes and counting our numbers, preparing the intelligence the main force needs to strike.

Hunt them down. Kill ten Sunhawk Spies and collect five of their insignias — we need evidence of who dispatched them and the scope of their mission.

nazzivian_demons.name: The Nazzivian Demons
nazzivian_demons.description:
Nazzivian, the old ruins in the northwest, has become a gathering place for satyrs and their demonic servants. These Nazzivus satyrs perform rituals that draw more void creatures to Bloodmyst Isle, compounding the existing corruption.

Raid Nazzivian. Kill ten of the Nazzivus Satyrs and six of the more powerful Felsworn, and recover five of their demonic relics so our mages can study their ritual methods.

the_void_anomalies.name: The Void Anomalies
the_void_anomalies.description:
The satyr rituals at Nazzivian have torn holes in reality itself, allowing void anomalies to seep through. These formless creatures of the Void amplify the existing corruption wherever they wander.

I need you to collapse these anomalies before they spread further. Destroy twelve void anomalies in and around Nazzivian and return to me.

wrathtail_menace.name: The Wrathtail Menace
wrathtail_menace.description:
The Wrathtail naga have fortified the Hidden Reef on Bloodmyst's northern coast. They arrived shortly after our crash, drawn by the corrupted mana energies. Now they blockade the sea lanes we need for eventual rescue.

Break their hold on the reef. Kill ten Wrathtail Naiads and six of their Wave Mistresses, then recover five of their scales. Our artificers believe the scales can be worked into durable armor.

razormaw_quest.name: Razormaw
razormaw_quest.description:
The Wrathtail naga keep an ancient sea serpent named Razormaw as their guardian beast. As long as that creature lives, the naga will be emboldened to hold the Hidden Reef and eventually push further inland.

Fight through the Wrathtail ranks to reach Razormaw in the depths of the reef, and slay it. Bring back one of its enormous teeth — I want those naga to know their guardian will not return.

sunhawk_assault.name: Assault on Sunhawk Base
sunhawk_assault.description:
The Sunhawk blood elves have established a fortified base in the southwest of Bloodmyst Isle. Their defenders and legionnaires provide muscle while their conjurers power the whole operation using mana siphoned from the Vector Coil.

We must cripple them. Kill ten Sunhawk Defenders, six Conjurers, and eight Legionnaires. Recover eight of their insignias as proof of how deep their operation runs on this island.

restoring_sanctity.name: Restoring Sanctity
restoring_sanctity.description:
The Vector Coil — the naaru power vessel that crashed with the Exodar — has been overrun by void constructs summoned by Zarakh the eredar. These guardians and bloodcrystal reavers have twisted a sacred site into something foul.

Fight your way to the Vector Coil's outer platform and destroy ten of the Vector Coil Guardians and eight Bloodcrystal Reavers. We must clear them before I can attempt to purge the corruption from the core.

zarakh_quest.name: Zarakh Must Fall
zarakh_quest.description:
Zarakh the eredar commands everything — the blood elves, the void constructs, all of it. He has come to Bloodmyst Isle to transform the Vector Coil into a weapon of the Burning Legion. If he succeeds, not only will we be destroyed, but the naaru's power will be turned against the Light itself.

Break through the remaining Sunhawk Harbingers protecting him and confront Zarakh at the Vector Coil's heart. Destroy him and bring back his sigil as proof that this nightmare has ended.

moonstalker_hunt.name: The Moonstalker Problem
moonstalker_hunt.description:
The moonstalker population along Darkshore's southern coast has grown dangerously large this season. Three fishermen from Auberdine were killed last week on the road to their boats, and the sentinels cannot spare the numbers to escort every traveler.

Kill twelve moonstalkers and bring me eight of their fangs. I'll craft them into something useful, and the road will be safer for it.

bear_and_tender_trouble.name: Bear and Tender Trouble
bear_and_tender_trouble.description:
Something has gone wrong in the forests north of Auberdine. The grizzled thistle bears that normally stay deep in the hills have begun wandering onto the road, and the grove tenders — the ancient treants who once protected these woods — are attacking travelers and animals alike.

Kill ten grizzled thistle bears and eight of the corrupted grove tenders. Collect five bear claws for our craftsmen while you work.

blackwood_corruption.name: The Blackwood Corruption
blackwood_corruption.description:
The Blackwood furbolg den to the northeast has gone silent. These furbolg were never our allies, but they kept to themselves — until recently. Now their warriors and ursa pour out of the den attacking anything that moves.

We need to push them back. Kill ten Blackwood Warriors and eight of their hulking Ursa before they overwhelm our eastern outpost.

cleansing_the_shrine.name: Cleansing the Shrine
cleansing_the_shrine.description:
Our scouts found a corrupted shrine at the heart of Blackwood Den that appears to be the source of the blight driving the furbolg to madness. The Blackwood Shamans perform their rituals around it, spreading the taint with each ceremony.

Kill eight Blackwood Shamans and eight Pathfinders guarding the shrine, and recover five of the corrupted stone tablets they carry. I need to study these inscriptions.

ruins_of_mathystra.name: Ruins of Mathystra
ruins_of_mathystra.description:
The Ruins of Mathystra hold the remnants of an ancient night elf civilization. Now the naga have moved in, digging through the rubble and carting off our ancestors' artifacts. The ruins golems that were supposed to keep them out apparently cannot distinguish naga from night elf.

Kill eight of the ruins golems and ten of the invading naga. Recover five of the relics the naga have already excavated so they can be properly preserved.

twilight_cultists.name: The Tower of Althalaxx
twilight_cultists.description:
The Tower of Althalaxx on Darkshore's western cliff is home to the Twilight Hammer cult. These cultists have been gathering power for months — their acolytes patrol the forest paths and waylay lone travelers.

Kill ten Twilight Acolytes in the area around the tower and recover five of their elemental invocation tablets. The more we understand about what they're doing here, the better prepared we'll be to stop it.

inside_the_tower.name: Into the Tower
inside_the_tower.description:
The acolytes outside the tower were only the outer ring. Inside, Athrikus Narassin's senior cultists — the Twilight Geolords — perform the true rituals that are slowly corrupting the elemental lines running beneath Darkshore.

Fight into the tower and kill ten Twilight Geolords. Disrupting their inner circle will weaken the wards protecting Athrikus himself.

athrikus_narassin_quest.name: Athrikus Narassin
athrikus_narassin_quest.description:
With his geolords weakened, Athrikus Narassin waits at the tower's summit. This warlock has plagued Darkshore for decades, and every attempt to remove him has failed. Not today.

Fight to the top of the Tower of Althalaxx through his remaining geolord guard, and end Athrikus Narassin once and for all. Bring me his head as proof that the tower has fallen.

shatterspear_trolls.name: The Shatterspear Trolls
shatterspear_trolls.description:
The Shatterspear trolls have been growing bolder. Their warriors have been attacking the northern road and their headhunters have started ranging as far south as the Ruins of Mathystra.

Fight into Shatterspear Vale and kill ten of their warriors and eight headhunters. Recover six of the hex dolls their witch doctors are binding to our sentinels — we need to break those curses before they cost more lives.

shatterspear_chieftain_quest.name: The Shatterspear Chieftain
shatterspear_chieftain_quest.description:
The Shatterspear Chieftain sits at the heart of the vale, rallying his people for what he calls a final war against the night elves of Auberdine. Without him, the tribe will fracture and return to their isolation.

Fight through his witch doctor guards and challenge the chieftain directly. Bring back his war drum — without it, no warrior of the Shatterspear will be willing to answer the call to battle.

ghostpaw_menace.name: The Ghostpaw Menace
ghostpaw_menace.description:
The Ghostpaw wolf packs are ranging further from their territory than ever before. Our sentinels have spotted them as far north as the Falfarren River, and two young hunters were attacked near the road.

Cull the packs — kill twelve Ghostpaw Runners and bring me eight of their pelts. The hides can be put to use, and fewer wolves on the roads will keep our patrols safer.

spitelash_river_threat.name: Spitelash River Threat
spitelash_river_threat.description:
The Spitelash naga have pushed deep into Ashenvale's river crossings, cutting off our supply routes from Astranaar to the northern outposts. Each day they hold the rivers, our position weakens.

Fight through them and bring me ten Spitelash crests as proof of the river lanes reclaimed. Every one you cut down is another day we hold Ashenvale.

foulweald_corruption.name: Foulweald Corruption
foulweald_corruption.description:
The Foulweald furbolg were once peaceful neighbors who tended the eastern reaches of Ashenvale alongside our druids. Now a dark corruption has turned them feral. Their scouts harry our roads and their warriors destroy anything that moves near the den.

Kill ten Foulweald Pathfinders and eight of the hulking Ursa warriors. Bring back eight of their ritual totems — our druids must study the corruption's source.

silence_the_shamans.name: Silence the Shamans
silence_the_shamans.description:
Our druids have confirmed it — the Foulweald shamans are the vector through which the corruption spreads from the den's dark heart into the surrounding tribe. Each shaman that falls weakens the blight's grip on the furbolg.

Seek out and destroy ten Foulweald Shamans inside their den. The totems they carry will dissolve without a shaman to channel them — but bring me six as proof that the rites have been disrupted.

warsong_supply_lines.name: Warsong Supply Lines
warsong_supply_lines.description:
The Warsong Clan is stripping Ashenvale bare. Every tree they fell is shipped north — and every axe thrower they post means another road we cannot use.

Destroy their supply line. Kill twelve Warsong Axe Throwers and ten Outriders, and collect twelve of their clan insignias. Without soldiers, the lumber operation stalls.

warsong_commander_quest.name: Disrupt the Command
warsong_commander_quest.description:
The Warsong Commanders carry the Warchief's direct orders and hold the lumber operation together with iron discipline. Without them, the remaining soldiers will falter and fall back.

Kill eight Warsong Commanders and their Shredder escorts. Bring back eight insignias from the commanders — proof the operation's spine has been broken.

xavian_enclave.name: The Xavian Enclave
xavian_enclave.description:
Xavian was once a night elf settlement before the Sundering drove the satyrs to claim its ruins as their own. Now it is a festering wound in Ashenvale's heart, and the demon runes they carve spread corruption into the surrounding forest.

Break through the enclave's outer ring. Kill twelve Xavian Betrayers and ten Skulkers, and collect ten of the demon runes they carry — our mages will use them to locate the ley-line tap powering the corruption.

lord_xavialis_quest.name: End of the Enclave
lord_xavialis_quest.description:
Lord Xavialis is the will behind the Xavian enclave's corruption. As long as the demon lord lives, the satyrs will never break and the corruption will keep spreading.

Fight through his skull guard and confront Lord Xavialis. Bring me his broken horn as proof of your victory.

raynewood_cleansing.name: Cleansing Raynewood
raynewood_cleansing.description:
Raynewood Retreat has fallen silent. The ancient treants that once tended its groves have gone mad, and the fel spirits drawn to the corruption are making it worse with each passing day.

Enter the retreat and banish ten Fel Spirits. Their essence, when purified, can serve as a reagent for the cleansing ritual our druids are preparing. Collect ten of the crystallized shards they leave behind.

ordanus_quest.name: Ordanus, Son of Cenarius
ordanus_quest.description:
Ordanus, a son of Cenarius himself, has been driven to madness by the fel corruption rotting the heart of Raynewood Retreat. He destroys all who enter — there is no reasoning with him now.

The druids have made their decision: Ordanus must fall. Fight through his treant guardians and face Ordanus. Bring me his eye as proof. It is the only way to stop the corruption from consuming all of Raynewood.

thunder_lizard_hunt.name: Thunder Lizard Hunt
thunder_lizard_hunt.description:
The Greater Thunder Lizards of Stonetalon have become a serious problem. Their thunderous footsteps shake the roads loose and their lightning discharges have struck two of our outriders this season.

Kill ten of the lizards roaming the mountain foothills and bring back eight of their scales. A leatherworker in the pass is studying the conductive properties of the scales — she'll pay well for what you collect.

scorpid_menace.name: Scorpid Menace
scorpid_menace.description:
Stoneclaw Scorpids have overrun the lower mountain pass between the foothills and the main road. Their numbers have tripled since last season, and the mountain lions that once kept them in check have retreated.

Kill twelve Stoneclaw Scorpids and eight Stonetalon Mountain Lions to restore the natural balance of the pass. Bring eight scorpid pincers as proof — I have a use for them.

venture_co_intrusion.name: Venture Co. Intrusion
venture_co_intrusion.description:
The Venture Company has moved into Windshear Crag in force, and their shredders are working day and night to strip the ancient trees. Every worker they field means more trees fall and more of Stonetalon becomes wasteland.

Hit their labor gangs. Kill twelve Venture Co. Workers and eight of their Geologists, and collect ten of their identification badges. Every operative you stop is one fewer pair of hands on those machines.

windshear_shutdown.name: Windshear Shutdown
windshear_shutdown.description:
The Venture Co. Foremen are the backbone of the Windshear Crag operation — without them the workers scatter and the shredders sit idle. And those shredders must go. Every one you destroy saves a hundred trees.

Kill eight of the Foremen and six of the Shredder crews. Bring back eight badges from the foremen — we need to account for every officer of that operation.

grimtotem_warpath.name: The Grimtotem Warpath
grimtotem_warpath.description:
The Grimtotem tauren have claimed the eastern post and consider every living being in Stonetalon an enemy. They've killed two night elf couriers and a gnome surveying team in the past month alone.

Fight your way into the post and cut down ten Grimtotem Warriors and eight of their Brutes. Bring me ten of their warclub fragments as proof of the threat reduced.

silence_the_grimtotem_totems.name: Silence the Dark Totems
silence_the_grimtotem_totems.description:
The Grimtotem shamans perform rites that bind the spirits of Stonetalon to the clan's cause — every totem they plant poisons more of the land around the post. If we can silence the shamans, the spirits may yet be freed.

Kill ten Grimtotem Shamans inside the post and collect eight of their spirit totems. Our druids will use the totems to study and reverse the corruption spreading from the rites.

charred_vale_expedition.name: Into the Charred Vale
charred_vale_expedition.description:
The Charred Vale was once the most beautiful forest in all of Stonetalon. Now it is ash and ember, and the spirits that haunt it grow more numerous with each passing year. If we do not act, the corruption will spread beyond the vale's natural borders.

Enter the Charred Vale and destroy ten Charred Spirits and ten Charred Basilisks. Collect eight basilisk scales — they contain residual fire energy that our mages need for the warding ritual.

charred_ancient_quest.name: The Charred Ancient
charred_ancient_quest.description:
There is an ancient of war in the deepest part of the Charred Vale — once a guardian of all Stonetalon, now a raging inferno in the shape of a tree. Our druids believe that as long as it burns, the vale cannot begin to heal.

Fight through the Magma Elementals that cluster around it and bring the Charred Ancient down. Return to me with its heart-ember — proof that the guardian's torment has ended and the vale can begin its long recovery.

bloodfury_assault.name: Bloodfury Assault
bloodfury_assault.description:
The Bloodfury harpy clan has claimed Stonetalon Peak as their domain, and they attack any who approach the summit. The Sentinel post at the peak has been under siege for weeks — relief supplies cannot get through.

Break the siege. Kill twelve Bloodfury Rippers and eight of the Windwitches, then collect ten harpy feathers as proof of the clan thinned. Do enough damage and the matriarch will emerge.

bloodfury_matriarch_quest.name: The Bloodfury Matriarch
bloodfury_matriarch_quest.description:
The Bloodfury Matriarch commands her clan from the highest spire of Stonetalon Peak. She will not stop the siege until she falls. The Sentinel commander has been blunt: kill the matriarch and the clan will scatter.

Fight through her windwitch guard and face the matriarch in her eyrie. Bring her talon back to the commander — that is the only proof she will accept that Stonetalon Peak is clear.

barrens_predator_cull.name: Barrens Predator Cull
barrens_predator_cull.description:
The raptors and lions of the southern Barrens have grown bold. Two supply wagons on the Gold Road were destroyed last week — one driver killed, one missing. The predators are raiding closer to camp with every passing day.

Kill twelve Southern Barrens Raptors and eight Barrens Lions. Bring back eight raptor claws to confirm the cull — the leatherworkers here can use them.

plainstrider_roundup.name: Plainstrider Roundup
plainstrider_roundup.description:
The Greater Plainstrider flocks are overrunning the southern grazing lands. Their size means they trample the scrub grass the kodo need for forage, and the tauren herd-masters have been complaining for months.

Kill twelve Greater Plainstriders and collect ten of their display plumes. The fletchers at camp can turn those plumes into arrows — nothing goes to waste on the Gold Road.

razorfen_advance.name: The Razorfen Advance
razorfen_advance.description:
The Razorfen quilboar are pushing their briar boundary south again. Their battleguards and wardens have been raiding tauren homesteads within a day's ride of Camp Taurajo, and each raid grows bolder than the last.

March into the Razorfen Fields and cut down twelve Battleguards and eight Wardens. Bring back ten of their snout rings — every ring is a fighter the Razorfen can't replace.

geomancer_rituals.name: Geomancer Rituals
geomancer_rituals.description:
The Razorfen geomancers are conducting something new — a ritual that extends the Overgrowth's briar roots further each week. If we don't stop it, the thorns will reach the Gold Road within the season.

Kill ten Geomancers and eight Thornweavers. Collect eight of their ritual masks — our shamans need to examine the focus enchantments to understand what the Razorfen are attempting.

bristleback_aggression.name: Bristleback Aggression
bristleback_aggression.description:
The Bristleback quilboar camp in the southern scrublands has doubled in size since last season. Their warriors have been hitting our outriders daily, and two tauren hunters were driven off their territory by Thornlords last week.

Break the camp's fighting strength. Kill twelve Bristleback Warriors and eight Thornlords, and bring back ten quills — proof the threat has been reduced.

shamanic_thorn_rites.name: Shamanic Thorn Rites
shamanic_thorn_rites.description:
The Bristleback shamans are performing thorn-binding rites that harden the camp's warriors for days at a time. Every shaman that performs those rites makes the entire camp deadlier.

Kill ten Bristleback Shamans and collect eight of their hardened quills — the ones they use as ritual instruments. Our shamans believe breaking the rite-chain will weaken the entire camp's fighting edge.

kolkar_warpath.name: The Kolkar Warpath
kolkar_warpath.description:
The Kolkar centaur have declared the southern Barrens their territory and backed that claim with raids on every tauren settlement between here and Thousand Needles. Their drudges and marauders ride in formation now — this is no longer banditry, it is conquest.

Hit the hold hard. Kill twelve Kolkar Drudges and ten Marauders. Collect ten of their war brands — each one is proof of a warrior who will never raid our roads again.

kolkar_khan_quest.name: The Kolkar Khan
kolkar_khan_quest.description:
The Kolkar Khan commands the southern warband from a fortified hold off the Gold Road. Three retaliatory strikes have failed to dislodge him — he simply waits behind his Stormseers while they call lightning from the sky.

Fight through his Stormseer guard and kill the Khan himself. Bring back his iron bracer — it is the warlord's seal of command, and without it the warband has no authority to hold that ground.

razorfen_kraul_assault.name: Into Razorfen Kraul
razorfen_kraul_assault.description:
The inner sanctum of Razorfen Kraul is where Charlga Razorflank conducts her death rites. Before you can reach her, the Defenders and Spearhides of the inner passage must be cleared.

Kill twelve Razorfen Defenders and eight Spearhides inside the Kraul, and collect eight snout rings from the defenders as proof of the passage cleared. Only then will the path to Charlga be open.

charlga_razorflank_quest.name: Charlga Razorflank
charlga_razorflank_quest.description:
Charlga Razorflank sits at the heart of Razorfen Kraul weaving death magic inherited from the Scourge. As long as she lives the Overgrowth grows, the dead walk, and the Razorfen have a purpose beyond raiding.

Fight through her Spearhide guard and confront Charlga in her sanctum. Return with her severed tusk — it is the only proof our shamans will accept that the death rites have ended.

swoop_menace.name: The Screeching Menace
swoop_menace.description:
The Screeching Swoops have driven our scouts off the canyon floor approach for three days running. One flock took down a kodo, which tells you all you need to know about how bold they've gotten.

Kill twelve Screeching Swoops and eight Needles Cougars while you're at it, and bring back eight screeching swoop gizzards. Reducing the flock size will reopen the canyon approach for our patrols.

saltstone_survey.name: Saltstone Survey
saltstone_survey.description:
The Saltstone Basilisks on the canyon floor are a hazard to every patrol we send toward the needle formations. Their petrification gaze has already claimed one of our scouts — we found him standing upright in the basin, stone-still, with a look of absolute surprise on his face.

Kill ten Saltstone Basilisks and collect eight of their crystalline eyes. Our shamans need to study the gaze mechanism so we can develop a counter.

galak_advance.name: The Galak Advance
galak_advance.description:
Galak Scouts and Outrunners have been ranging further from their encampments every week — our supply riders on the canyon route have been hit four times in a fortnight. The Galak are testing us, seeing how much they can take before we push back.

Push back. Kill twelve Galak Scouts and ten Outrunners and bring back ten of their brands. Let the clan count their dead and reconsider their range.

clear_the_canyon.name: Clear the Canyon
clear_the_canyon.description:
Screeching Canyon is choked with Whitefeather Swoopers and Canyon Rock Elementals. Until both are thinned, we cannot use the canyon as a supply route or staging ground — the swoopers dive on anything that moves and the elementals simply walk through our barricades.

Kill ten Whitefeather Swoopers and eight Canyon Rock Elementals. Collect six more swoop gizzards while you're in there — the alchemists still need them.

galak_warband.name: The Galak Warband
galak_warband.description:
The Galak encampment at the heart of Thousand Needles is the source of every raid on our roads. Windchasers break our lines and Maulers finish what's left. The warband needs to know that we can reach them in their own camp.

Kill twelve Galak Windchasers and ten Maulers and bring back ten of their brands. Hit the main encampment, not the outlying scouts — we need the clan to feel this.

galak_storm_magic.name: Galak Storm Magic
galak_storm_magic.description:
The Galak Stormers are the most dangerous fighters in the main encampment — their chain lightning can hit three of our warriors with one discharge. They carry charged storm crystals that act as magical capacitors, and without those crystals the stormers are just ordinary centaur.

Kill ten Galak Stormers and collect eight of their storm crystals. Our shamans can study them to develop lightning-warding totems for the Freewind Post garrison.

darkcloud_assault.name: Darkcloud Assault
darkcloud_assault.description:
Darkcloud Pinnacle is held by the most hostile sept of the Grimtotem clan. They attack tauren, Horde, and centaur indiscriminately — anyone who approaches the base of their needle gets a war party down the cliff face in minutes.

Assault the lower passages. Kill ten Grimtotem Raiders and ten Stompers, and collect ten of their pinnacle relics — we need the Elder Crone to know that even her sanctum is not safe.

elder_crone_quest.name: The Elder Crone
elder_crone_quest.description:
The Grimtotem Elder Crone has held Darkcloud Pinnacle for longer than anyone alive can remember. Her dark totemic magic protects the summit and warps the stone itself against attackers. She must be removed — her influence corrupts every Grimtotem who comes within range of her rites.

Fight through her Elder Shaman guard to the summit and bring the Crone down. Return her spirit-fetish as proof. It is the anchor of her power — without it, her hold over the pinnacle ends.

galak_hunters.name: Galak Hunters
galak_hunters.description:
The Wranglers and Bloodseekers of Splithoof Hold are the Galak High Warlord's personal guard and enforcement arm. Bloodseekers operate under blood oaths — they will chase targets across the entire zone if it comes to it.

Hit the hold's inner ranks. Kill ten Galak Wranglers and ten Bloodseekers and collect ten brands. Thin the elite guard before the final push on the High Warlord himself.

galak_high_warlord_quest.name: The Galak High Warlord
galak_high_warlord_quest.description:
The Galak High Warlord commands the centaur conquest of Thousand Needles from Splithoof Hold. While he lives, the Galak will not stop — they follow his blood oath to the last centaur. He has to fall.

Fight through his Bloodseeker bodyguard and face the High Warlord. Bring back his war standard — planted at Splithoof Hold's gate as a declaration of Galak dominion. When it falls, the conquest ends.

scorpashi_menace.name: Scorpashi Menace
scorpashi_menace.description:
The scorpashi of Desolace have been ranging further into the caravan routes, and three supply wagons were lost to their attacks this month alone. The outriders cannot thin their numbers fast enough.

Head into the grey plains and kill twelve Scorpashi Snappers. Bring back ten of their pincers — the leatherworkers can use them, and it proves the cull was done properly.

kodo_graveyard.name: Kodo Graveyard
kodo_graveyard.description:
Desolace's kodo herds have roamed this land since before the Sundering, and the Horde needs them alive. Hunters have been slaughtering them for sport, and the population is thinning dangerously.

Take eight kodo hooves from the bulls that wander the plains — culling only the sick and injured — so we can track which bloodlines are dying out. Bring them to me.

burning_blade_disruption.name: Burning Blade Disruption
burning_blade_disruption.description:
The Burning Blade cult has taken root in Mannoroc Coven, and their rituals are tearing holes in the barrier between this world and the Twisting Nether. They must be stopped before the breach widens beyond any hope of closing.

Kill fourteen Burning Blade Cultists and ten of their Warlocks. Bring back twelve of their demonic seals to show the ritual circles have been broken.

shadows_of_mannoroc.name: Shadows of Mannoroc
shadows_of_mannoroc.description:
The demons summoned at Mannoroc Coven leave behind a residue of pure fel energy when slain — demon dust that the Burning Blade uses to power their darkest rituals. Deny them the resource.

Kill the lesser and greater demons prowling the coven and collect twenty measures of Mannoroc Demon Dust from the bodies. Scatter it to the wind so it cannot be reclaimed.

magram_aggression.name: Magram Aggression
magram_aggression.description:
The Magram centaur have been raiding caravans along the southern routes and are pushing closer to our outpost with each passing week. We cannot maintain supply lines while they control the passes.

Ride into their territory and cut down fourteen Magram Marauders and ten of their Stormers. Bring back sixteen of their horns — they use them as war horns to call reinforcements, and taking them weakens their communications.

gelkis_skirmishers.name: Gelkis Skirmishers
gelkis_skirmishers.description:
The Gelkis centaur control the Valley of Spears and have been attacking anything that crosses their territory — our scouts, merchants, and shamans alike. They are just as dangerous as the Magram and must be answered.

Fight your way into the valley and kill twelve Gelkis Outrunners and ten of their Maulers. Return with sixteen of their horns as proof the patrols have been broken up.

shadows_of_sargeron.name: Shadows of Sargeron
shadows_of_sargeron.description:
The ruins of Sargeron were once a night elf waystation. Now satyrs haunt its crumbled towers, conducting shadow rituals that grow bolder with each dark moon. Their shadow gems are the focal point of these rites — destroying them will cripple their power.

Kill twelve Satyr Shadowsages and recover ten of their shadow gems. Shatter the gems when you have them — their dark energy will disperse harmlessly in sunlight.

haunted_wastes.name: Haunted Wastes
haunted_wastes.description:
The grey plains of Desolace are plagued with wandering spirits — echoes of creatures slain in battles long forgotten. They attack the living with mindless fury, and our outriders cannot cross the wastes safely while they roam.

Hunt down sixteen of the Desolace Ghosts that haunt the open plains and put them to rest permanently. The elements willing, their energy will dissipate and the wastes will be safe to cross once more.

magram_overlord_quest.name: The Magram Overlord
magram_overlord_quest.description:
The Magram Overlord is the warmaster who holds the entire clan together — without him, their raiding parties will collapse into infighting and scatter. He camps in the heart of Magram Village, surrounded by his elite guard.

Fight your way through his Stormers and bring the Overlord down. Return with his skull as proof — the Horde must know the Magram threat has been answered.

maraudon_deep_quest.name: The Earth Below Maraudon
maraudon_deep_quest.description:
Deep within Maraudon, past the corrupted sprites and stone golems, an ancient earth elemental called Landslide has awakened. It was once a dormant elemental node — now it erupts with destructive rage, collapsing passages and crushing any who venture too deep.

Fight through the stone golems guarding the inner passages and confront Landslide. Bring back a fragment of its shattered core as proof it has been defeated.

bloodfen_culling.name: Bloodfen Culling
bloodfen_culling.description:
The Bloodfen raptors have become a serious problem. They've taken three of our outriders this season and are breeding faster than we can thin them. The wetlands around Bloodfen are impassable while they hold the territory.

Kill fourteen Bloodfen Raptors and eight of the larger Screamers. Bring back twelve of their claws to confirm the cull was carried out — the leatherworkers here can put them to good use.

darkmist_infestation.name: Darkmist Infestation
darkmist_infestation.description:
The Darkmist ruins are overrun with widows and their broods. A scouting party we sent three days ago hasn't reported back, and I fear the worst. The silk those spiders spin is extraordinarily strong — we can use it, but only after the nest is thinned.

Kill twelve Darkmist Widows and eliminate twenty of their Hatchlings before they mature. Bring back ten coils of spider silk from the widows.

snapjaw_menace.name: Snapjaw Menace
snapjaw_menace.description:
The crocolisk population in the marsh channels has exploded this season. They've capsized two supply rafts and killed four workers at the river crossing. We need them pushed back before we lose the waterway entirely.

Kill ten Darkwater Crocolisks and eight of the larger Snapjaws. Bring back twelve scales — the armorsmiths can turn them into proper plating.

dustwallow_hyena_packs.name: Dustwallow Hyena Packs
dustwallow_hyena_packs.description:
The hyena packs roaming the Bloodfen wetlands have been following our outriders, picking off stragglers and injured riders. A large cackle has established a den near the main trail and attacks anything that passes after dark.

Break up the packs — kill sixteen hyenas and bring back their fangs as proof. The den needs to be cleared before it gets any bigger.

grimscale_threat.name: Grimscale Threat
grimscale_threat.description:
The Grimscale murlocs have been raiding the southern shoreline settlements and ambushing fishing boats in the channels. Their oracles have been using their totems to coordinate attacks across the whole tribe — destroy the totems and you break their ability to organize.

Kill fourteen Grimscale Murlocs and collect twelve of their ritual totems. Bring them back so we can study how their coordination magic works.

theramore_deserters.name: Theramore Deserters
theramore_deserters.description:
Theramore deserters have been raiding supply convoys along the marsh roads and ambushing anyone who ventures too far from the main settlements. Their conjurors are particularly dangerous — they can bring down riders from a hundred yards.

Hunt down twelve of the Deserters and eight of their Conjurors. Collect sixteen of their insignias — Theramore command wants them returned to make a formal record of who went rogue.

swampwalker_corruption.name: Swampwalker Corruption
swampwalker_corruption.description:
The swampwalkers that guard the deep groves of Dustwallow have turned hostile. They once kept to themselves and left travelers alone — now they uproot and attack anything that enters their territory. Something has corrupted them.

We cannot let the grove become completely impassable. Fight through and cut down fourteen Swampwalkers and eight of the massive Swampwalker Elders that lead them.

dragon_whelp_culling.name: Dragon Whelp Culling
dragon_whelp_culling.description:
The black dragon whelps nesting in the Wyrmbog are growing in number every season. Left unchecked, they'll be large enough to pose a threat to the entire region within the year. Each whelp that reaches adulthood is another fully grown black dragon we'll have to deal with later.

Enter the Wyrmbog and kill sixteen Black Dragon Whelps and eight of the maturing Scalebanes before they can grow any further.

grimscale_high_oracle_quest.name: The High Oracle's Idol
grimscale_high_oracle_quest.description:
The Grimscale High Oracle is the source of every coordinated raid the tribe has launched against us. As long as she leads them and channels the tidal currents through that idol of hers, the Grimscale will keep coming.

Fight through her guardian oracles and confront the High Oracle herself. Return with her idol — proof that the tribe's spiritual power has been broken.

wyrmbog_matriarch_quest.name: The Wyrmbog Matriarch
wyrmbog_matriarch_quest.description:
The Wyrmbog Matriarch is an ancient black dragon who has ruled the bog for decades. She is the reason the whelp population keeps growing — every season she produces another clutch of eggs, and every clutch we don't destroy becomes another generation of black dragons threatening Dustwallow.

Fight through her whelp guard and bring the matriarch down. Return with one of her obsidian scales as proof — a trophy that will tell every black dragon in the region that Dustwallow is no longer theirs.

frayfeather_hunt.name: Frayfeather Hunt
frayfeather_hunt.description:
The hippogryphs of Feralas are unique to this jungle, and their feathers hold a flight enchantment used by Feathermoon fletchers. We need them, but we also need the hippogryph population kept in check — they've been diving at riders on the forest roads.

Hunt twelve Feralas Hippogryphs and collect ten of their primary feathers. The fletchers will put every one to good use.

sprite_darter_menace.name: Sprite Darter Menace
sprite_darter_menace.description:
The Sprite Darter Nymphs that drift through the Feralas jungle have been attacking druid apprentices and lone travelers near the forest paths. They move in swarms when disturbed, and their nature bolts are strong enough to knock a rider from a mount.

Kill fourteen Sprite Darter Nymphs and bring back twelve of their wing membranes. The membranes can be used to craft arcane dampeners that will help protect our people.

the_feral_scar.name: The Feral Scar
the_feral_scar.description:
The Feral Scar yetis have expanded their territory beyond the cave network and are attacking anything on the jungle floor. Two of our trackers were killed this week. The yetis are far too aggressive to coexist with the supply routes we need through this area.

Kill fourteen Feral Scar Yetis and bring back twelve pelts. The fur can be turned into insulating armor, and the kills will push the survivors back into the deeper caves.

gordunni_rune_stones.name: Gordunni Rune Stones
gordunni_rune_stones.description:
The Gordunni ogres have been using their rune stones to expand territorial claims across the jungle. Their shamans empower the stones to repel our scouts. If we understand what the runes mean, we can counter them.

Fight through the ogre camps and collect sixteen rune stones from the Gordunni Ogres and Maulers. Bring them back — our scholars can decipher the markings and turn their own magic against them.

the_woodpaw_den.name: The Woodpaw Den
the_woodpaw_den.description:
The Woodpaw gnolls have built a sprawling den network through the southern jungle and are raiding supply caches and ambushing patrols at night. Their pack runners are too fast for our outriders to catch in the undergrowth.

Hit them on their own ground — kill fourteen Woodpaw Pack Runners and ten of their Alphas. Bring back twenty gnoll teeth as trophies. Every tooth taken is one less predator on the forest roads.

frayfeather_raiders.name: Frayfeather Raiders
frayfeather_raiders.description:
The Frayfeather harpies have been raiding our highland supply lines and nesting on the cliff faces above the main pass. Their Screechers can disable a rider with a single shriek, leaving them helpless against the rest of the flock.

Drive into the highlands and kill twelve Frayfeather Harpies and ten of their Screechers. Bring back eighteen wing tips — the fletchers and engineers can use the quills in a dozen different ways.

grimtotem_aggression.name: Grimtotem Aggression
grimtotem_aggression.description:
Grimtotem tauren have established war camps in Feralas and are attacking anyone who isn't one of them. Their shamans are the real danger — they keep warriors in the fight long past when they should fall and call down lightning on anyone who tries to flank.

Fight your way through their camps and kill sixteen Grimtotem Warriors and ten of their Shamans. Make it clear that Feralas is not their territory to claim.

the_giant_problem.name: The Giant Problem
the_giant_problem.description:
The cliff and mountain giants of Frayfeather Highlands have been pushing closer to the lowland paths. One mountain giant alone destroyed a campsite that took two weeks to build. We cannot operate in the highlands while they roam freely at the valley edges.

Kill ten Cliff Giants and six Mountain Giants. They won't retreat — they need to be put down.

gordunni_warchief_quest.name: The Gordunni Warchief
gordunni_warchief_quest.description:
The Gordunni Warchief is the warlord keeping the entire tribe unified. Take him down and the ogres will fracture into warring sub-clans too busy fighting each other to threaten anyone else. He keeps his inner guard close, but they're maulers, not strategists.

Fight through his guard and bring the Warchief down. His skull is the proof we need that the Gordunni threat has been answered.

immol_thar_quest.name: Immol'thar
immol_thar_quest.description:
Deep within Dire Maul, the demon Immol'thar has been freed from the bindings the Highborne placed on him millennia ago. He has been feeding on ley line energy ever since and growing more powerful with every hour. If he is not destroyed, the fel corruption he is spreading will consume the entire ruin — and eventually the jungle around it.

Fight through his treant guardians and the arcane golems of the inner sanctum. Bring back a shard of Immol'thar's crystallized essence as proof that the demon is dead.

wastewander_threat.name: Wastewander Threat
wastewander_threat.description:
The Wastewander outlaws have been raiding every caravan attempting to cross Tanaris. Gadgetzan's trade routes are strangled. I've found journals that confirm their leader is coordinating ambushes from the ruins at Waterspring Field.

Cut down twelve of their Bandits and eight of their Rogues. Bring me eight of their journals so we can map their operation and shut it down for good.

basilisk_problem.name: Eyes of the Basilisk
basilisk_problem.description:
The Stonescale Basilisks have multiplied beyond all reckoning. Three of my scouts failed to return last week — I found what was left of them. Statues.

Hunters who know this land can sell basilisk eyes, but I need ten to fuel a research project that may prevent more petrification deaths. Kill the basilisks spreading through the eastern ruins and collect ten of their eyes.

southsea_menace.name: The Southsea Menace
southsea_menace.description:
The Southsea Freebooters have fortified Lost Rigger Cove into a proper pirate base. Ships can no longer safely pass the eastern coast — two supply vessels have been taken this month alone.

Sail into the cove and cut down ten Privateers and eight Freebooters. Recover eight of their doubloons as proof of how deeply they've dug into Tanaris.

elemental_disruption.name: Elemental Disruption
elemental_disruption.description:
Something is pulling the fire and water elementals of Tanaris into an aggressive frenzy. The Blazerunners are burning the ruins and the Tidewalkers are flooding the shoreline caves.

Until we understand the cause, we must contain them. Kill ten Blazerunners near the southern reaches and eight Tidewalkers along Southbreak Shore.

silithid_culling.name: The Silithid Threat
silithid_culling.description:
The silithid hive in southern Tanaris is expanding at an alarming rate. Scouts report wasp swarms two miles from the perimeter — they've never ranged that far before.

I need someone willing to go into the hive perimeter and cull the numbers before they become unstoppable. Kill twelve Silithid Wasps and ten Silithid Creepers, then bring back ten chitin shards to confirm the damage you've dealt.

sandfury_drums.name: Silence the War Drums
sandfury_drums.description:
The Sandfury trolls beat their war drums night and day from outside Zul'Farrak's walls, rallying more of their kind from across the desert. Every dawn the drums sound, more warriors answer the call.

We need to break their war council. Cut down twelve Sandfury Trolls and eight of their Axe Throwers. Recover eight of their war drums to silence the constant summoning.

dunemaul_subjugation.name: Dunemaul Subjugation
dunemaul_subjugation.description:
The Dunemaul clan's compound sits astride the only safe path through the northern passes of Tanaris. Every caravan must either pay their extortionate toll or fight through them.

Reduce their numbers and their appetite for extortion. Kill twelve Dunemaul Ogres and eight of their Warlocks. Bring back eight hide scraps to confirm you've put a dent in their operation.

hyena_packs.name: Tanaris Hyena Packs
hyena_packs.description:
The Desert Hyenas have moved out of the deep desert and started picking off the goblins' pack animals near the Waterspring ruins. Three caravans have lost their brahmin in the last two weeks.

Hunt down fifteen Desert Hyenas roaming the central basin. Their numbers need thinning before they grow bold enough to attack inside the ruins themselves.

sandfury_high_priest_quest.name: The High Priest's Idol
sandfury_high_priest_quest.description:
The Sandfury High Priest performs dark rites at Zul'Farrak's outer altars, channeling power that keeps the entire tribe in a state of frenzied devotion. As long as he operates freely, the Sandfury will never be broken.

Fight through his Warden guard and bring the High Priest to battle. Return with his sacred idol as proof that the source of the Sandfury's power has been severed.

gahzrilla_quest.name: Gahz'rilla
gahzrilla_quest.description:
Deep within Zul'Farrak lies a sacred pool. The Sandfury trolls use the Sacred Mallet to summon an ancient frost hydra — Gahz'rilla — to destroy those who have breached the inner sanctum.

Fight through the Axe Throwers guarding the pool's approach. When Gahz'rilla rises, bring it down. Return with one of its frost-scales as proof that the hydra's reign over Zul'Farrak's depths is ended.

surveyor_work.name: Surveyor Work
surveyor_work.description:
The blood elves have dispatched survey teams across the ruins of Azshara, cataloguing and reclaiming ancient Highborne relics. Their presence is becoming a serious problem — more teams arrive each week.

Cut down ten Blood Elf Surveyors and eight of their Reclaimers who guard the excavation sites. Collect eight of their sigils so we can track which operations we've disrupted.

arcanist_threat.name: The Arcanist Threat
arcanist_threat.description:
The Blood Elf Arcanists are the real danger. Their battle-mages can disrupt ley lines to fuel devastating arcane attacks, and they've already incinerated two of our messengers who came to assess the situation.

Eliminate twelve Blood Elf Arcanists from the excavation zones before they complete whatever ritual they're preparing.

naga_menace.name: Naga of the Shattered Strand
naga_menace.description:
The naga have locked down the entire eastern coastline of Azshara. Nothing passes the Shattered Strand without their approval — and their answer is always attack.

Fight through twelve Strand Crawlers and eight Naga Sirens. Recover eight of their coral idols to give the scholars a chance to understand what the naga are guarding along this shore.

hippogryph_territorial.name: Hippogryph Territorial
hippogryph_territorial.description:
The hippogryphs of Azshara have expanded their nesting territory into the main travel routes, making safe passage nearly impossible. Three messengers have been mauled this season alone.

Drive back their numbers. Kill fifteen Azshara Hippogryphs and eight of the larger Thunderheads roosting on the upper cliffs.

highborne_remnants.name: Highborne Remnants
highborne_remnants.description:
The Highborne spirits haunting the Ruins of Eldarath are not simply harmless ghosts. They attack any who approach, hurling arcane bolts from the ruins' inner chambers.

Clear the outer ruins of ten Highborne Apparitions and eight of the more powerful Lichlings. Collect eight of the runestones they guard — our loremasters believe they hold clues to what drove the Highborne mad.

satyr_corruption.name: Satyr Corruption
satyr_corruption.description:
The Legash satyrs are not merely squatting in Azshara — they are actively corrupting the ancient ley lines that run beneath the ruins. If they succeed, the arcane contamination could spread north for hundreds of miles.

Assault the Legash Encampment and kill twelve satyrs and eight of their Darkweavers. Bring back eight of their horns as proof of the ritual circle's leaders being eliminated.

dragon_culling.name: Dragon Culling
dragon_culling.description:
The azure dragons of the Bay of Storms have begun ranging inland, attacking anything that moves along the cliffs. The whelps are manageable, but the Scalebanes have started hunting in packs.

Cull twelve Azure Whelps from the shoreline and eight Azure Scalebanes from the cliff faces. Bring back ten scales so the Cenarion druids can study the storm-magic in their hide.

crocolisk_cull.name: Crocolisk Cull
crocolisk_cull.description:
The Riptide Crocolisks have completely taken over the tidal shallows between the Shattered Strand and the cliffs, and they've grown bold enough to drag people out of shallow water. The naga don't bother controlling them and neither will we — they need to be thinned.

Kill fifteen Riptide Crocolisks lurking in the coastal shallows and ten Azsharan Wolves roaming the cliffs above.

strand_warlord_quest.name: Warlord of the Shattered Strand
strand_warlord_quest.description:
Every naga expedition from the Shattered Strand is commanded by the same warlord — a towering serpent warrior whose orders have driven every recovery attempt from the coast for years. The naga will not break while he leads them.

Fight through his Sea Witch bodyguard and bring the Shattered Strand Warlord to battle. Return with his trident as proof of his defeat.

elder_azure_drake_quest.name: The Elder Azure Drake
elder_azure_drake_quest.description:
At the heart of the Bay of Storms lurks a dragon of ancient fury — an Elder Azure Drake whose mastery of storm and sea makes the entire bay lethal to approach. Scouts report it has begun constructing a lair that would make the Bay of Storms permanently impassable.

Fight through the Scalebanes who guard its approach and bring the Elder Azure Drake down. Return with one of its eyes as proof the bay can once again be crossed.

winterfall_aggression.name: Winterfall Aggression
winterfall_aggression.description:
The Winterfall furbolg were once peaceful — Timbermaw Hold still mourns them. Now they are utterly mad, attacking anyone who comes near their village in the central snowfields. Something is driving their corruption and it has to be stopped one warrior at a time.

Kill twelve Winterfall Warriors and eight Den Watchers, and recover eight of their corrupted totems so our shamans can study what dark influence has twisted them.

ruins_of_keltheril_quest.name: Ruins of Kel'Theril
ruins_of_keltheril_quest.description:
Beneath the frozen surface of Lake Kel'Theril lie the ruins of an ancient Highborne city. The spirits trapped there are in agony — they lash out at the living without thought or mercy.

Our loremasters need runestones from the ruins to understand what binds the spirits to this place. Kill ten of the Apparitions and eight of the Lichlings haunting the lakeside, and recover eight runestones from among them.

yeti_culling.name: Yeti Culling
yeti_culling.description:
The Ice Thistle yeti population has exploded this winter. They've moved into the central snowfields in numbers never seen before — three trade caravans have been destroyed in the last month.
