Cull the herd. Kill twelve Ice Thistle Yeti and eight of the massive Matriarchs who lead them. Bring back ten pelts to prove the scale of what you've accomplished.

chimera_pursuit.name: Chillwind Chimera
chimera_pursuit.description:
The Chillwind Chimera have become a lethal hazard on the frozen reaches. Their two-headed strikes come from unexpected angles and they've developed a taste for goblin trade caravans specifically.

Hunt down fifteen Chillwind Chimera and collect ten of their horns. The alchemists in Everlook have offered a substantial bounty for chimera horn, and their interest funds our operations here.

cobalt_dragon_culling.name: The Cobalt Ridge
cobalt_dragon_culling.description:
The cobalt blue drakes who nest on the northern ridges have begun venturing south into the trade lanes. The Cobalt Mageweavers are especially dangerous — their lightning barrages have vaporized more than one fully-armored patrol.

Push them back. Kill twelve Cobalt Scalebanes and eight Mageweavers. Return with ten wyrm scales so our armorers can study the lightning-conducting properties of their hide.

frostmaul_subjugation.name: Frostmaul Subjugation
frostmaul_subjugation.description:
The Frostmaul Giants have blockaded the approach to Darkwhisper Gorge. They answer to nothing and no one — they simply destroy whatever comes near. Researchers who need access to the gorge cannot reach it while the giants hold the pass.

Break the blockade. Kill twelve Frostmaul Giants and bring back twelve of their knuckle-bones as proof of how many you've put down.

frostsaber_territorial.name: Frostsaber Territorial
frostsaber_territorial.description:
The Frostsabers have been emboldened by the furbolg unrest in the central snowfields — without the Winterfall controlling the hunting grounds, the cats have expanded their territory aggressively. The Cobalt Wyverns circling overhead aren't helping.

Kill fifteen Frostsabers from the highland slopes and ten Cobalt Wyverns from the cliffs above the ruins.

darkwhisper_corruption.name: Darkwhisper Corruption
darkwhisper_corruption.description:
Something within Darkwhisper Gorge is pushing demonic influence outward into Winterspring. The Abyssals are ranging further than demons of their type ever should, and the Terrordars have begun to appear in areas previously considered safe.

Drive back the demon vanguard. Kill twelve Darkwhisper Abyssals and eight Darkwhisper Terrordars before they establish permanent footholds outside the gorge.

winterfall_high_shaman_quest.name: The Winterfall High Shaman
winterfall_high_shaman_quest.description:
Every Winterfall shaman answers to the High Shaman. His drumbeats carry the corrupting signal that keeps the entire tribe in their murderous frenzy. Silence the drum and the corruption loses its conductor.

Fight through his Shaman bodyguard and bring the Winterfall High Shaman down. Bring back his sacred drum — the symbol of his authority over the tribe.

darkwhisper_lord_quest.name: The Darkwhisper Lord
darkwhisper_lord_quest.description:
At the deepest point of Darkwhisper Gorge lurks a demon lord of ancient power, the source of the corruption spreading through Winterspring. As long as it endures, the Winterfall furbolg will remain mad and the gorge will keep pushing demons outward.

Fight through his Terrordar guard and destroy the Darkwhisper Lord. Return with the fel crystal shard anchoring him to this world — without it, his hold on Winterspring is broken.

angerclaw_threat.name: Angerclaw Threat
angerclaw_threat.description:
The Angerclaw bears of Felwood have been driven to killing frenzy by the fel corruption saturating the forest. They've pushed out of the thickets into the main travel paths and attacked three separate groups trying to pass through.

Cut down twelve Angerclaw Grizzlies and eight of the larger Maulers. Bring back ten clumps of their fur so we can track where the worst corruption concentrations are.

felwood_wolf_culling.name: Corrupted Wolves
felwood_wolf_culling.description:
The wolves of Felwood have been completely overtaken by the corruption. They no longer hunt strategically — they simply attack and kill without pattern, making them more dangerous than ever to anyone moving through the forest.

Kill fifteen Felwood Vile Wolves wherever you find them ranging through the southern thickets.

wildkin_menace.name: Wildkin Menace
wildkin_menace.description:
The Wildkin of Felwood were once sacred to Elune — her owlbeasts, keepers of the forest's sacred groves. Now the fel corruption has severed their connection to the moon goddess and left them as raging, mindless hulks.

Kill twelve Corrupted Wildkin and recover ten of their moon feathers. The Cenarion Circle believes the feathers may still carry traces of Elune's blessing worth studying.

deadwood_corruption.name: Deadwood Corruption
deadwood_corruption.description:
The Deadwood furbolg were once peaceful neighbors of the Timbermaw. Now they are wholly consumed by the fel — their shamans actively channel the corruption rather than resist it, accelerating the forest's destruction.

Kill twelve Deadwood Pathfinders and eight of their Avengers. Recover eight of their corrupted totems so the Cenarion druids can analyze how far the corruption has progressed.

irontree_blight.name: Irontree Blight
irontree_blight.description:
The Irontree ancients were once the oldest living things in Felwood, their roots stretching deep enough to touch ley lines older than night elf civilization. The corruption that claimed them is therefore especially catastrophic.

Kill ten Irontree Wanderers and eight Irontree Stompers. Collect ten vials of their corrupted sap — the Cenarion Circle needs samples to understand whether cleansing these ancients is even possible.

jadefire_menace.name: Jadefire Menace
jadefire_menace.description:
The Jadefire satyrs have established Jadefire Run as a demonic stronghold in the heart of Felwood, using it as a staging ground to push corruption deeper into the forest. Their idols mark the boundary of their claimed territory.

Assault Jadefire Run. Kill twelve Jadefire Satyrs and eight of the more dangerous Felsworn. Recover eight of their demonic idols as proof of the operation's scope.

corrupted_grove.name: The Corrupted Grove
corrupted_grove.description:
The deepest part of Irontree Woods has produced something worse than the Wanderers and Stompers — Withered Ancients so consumed by the fel that the corruption is spreading outward from their roots, killing the ground itself.

Destroy fifteen of the Withered Ancients before their root systems expand the blighted ground any further into the surrounding forest.

jaedenar_infiltration.name: Jaedenar Infiltration
jaedenar_infiltration.description:
Shadow Hold — the underground complex beneath Jaedenar — is the operational heart of the Shadow Council in Felwood. Every act of corruption in this forest can be traced back to orders issued from within those walls.

Kill twelve Jaedenar Cultists and eight of their Warlocks. Recover eight of their identifying sigils — the Council uses them to track loyalty, and they'll tell us how large this operation has grown.

jadefire_warlord_quest.name: The Jadefire Warlord
jadefire_warlord_quest.description:
The Jadefire Warlord coordinates every demonic incursion from Jadefire Run into the surrounding forest. As long as he commands, the satyrs will never stop pushing. He must be removed.

Fight through his Felsworn bodyguard and bring the Jadefire Warlord to battle. Return with his war horn — the symbol of his authority over Jadefire Run.

jaedenar_shadow_lord_quest.name: The Jaedenar Shadow Lord
jaedenar_shadow_lord_quest.description:
At the deepest level of Shadow Hold rules the Jaedenar Shadow Lord — the Shadow Council's master in Felwood. Every demon summoned, every ancient corrupted, every furbolg driven mad traces back to his orders.

Fight through his Houndmaster elite guard and destroy the Shadow Lord. Return with his seal — when the Shadow Council sees it in our hands, they will know their Felwood operation is finished.

ravasaur_hunt.name: Ravasaur Hunt
ravasaur_hunt.description:
The ravasaurs around Marshal's Refuge have grown bold. They've already claimed two supply runners this month and the patrols can't keep them at bay.

Cull their numbers — kill twelve ravasaurs and bring me eight of their claws as proof. Showing them the cost of hunting near our camp is the only language they understand.

gorilla_territory.name: Gorilla Territory
gorilla_territory.description:
The Un'Goro gorillas near the crater entry have expanded their territory into the supply paths we use to move goods. They don't discriminate — anything on two legs gets charged.

Displace them. Kill ten gorillas and bring me seven pelts to prove you drove them back. We'll treat the pelts and use them as signal markers.

pterrordax_culling.name: Pterrordax Culling
pterrordax_culling.description:
The pterrordax that nest on the crater walls have started targeting our scouts from the air. They're fast, agile, and they've learned to wait until a traveler is alone.

Bring me six pterrordax wings. The material has research value, and thinning their nesting population will keep the skies safer.

tar_pit_research.name: Tar Pit Research
tar_pit_research.description:
The Lakkari Tar Pits hold elementals unlike anything found elsewhere in Kalimdor. Their composition contains mineral compounds that could advance our understanding of elemental binding.

Kill eight tar lurkers and collect six tar samples. Handle the samples carefully — raw volcanic tar from a living elemental remains active long after extraction.

bloodpetal_infestation.name: Bloodpetal Infestation
bloodpetal_infestation.description:
The bloodpetal groves are spreading. These carnivorous plants have choked off three paths through the crater and claimed a campsite last week. Left unchecked, the entire southern passage will be impassable.

Kill ten bloodpetal lashers and trappers and collect eight of their seeds before they can propagate further.

silithid_clearing.name: Silithid Clearing
silithid_clearing.description:
The Slithering Scar silithid colony has been expanding its foraging range. Workers are appearing miles from the hive now — a sign that the queen is driving expansion.

Kill twelve silithid and bring back eight mandibles. If we can thin the worker caste, the colony will be forced to contract. Return before the ravagers start coordinating.

fire_plume_survey.name: Fire Plume Survey
fire_plume_survey.description:
Fire Plume Ridge was once a quiet volcanic vent. Something has agitated the elemental presence there — fire elementals are more numerous and aggressive than our last survey recorded.

Kill ten fire plume elementals and ragers to drive them back from the ridge approaches. I need to know if the activity is seasonal or something worse.

prehistoric_culling.name: Prehistoric Culling
prehistoric_culling.description:
The stegodons and stompers that roam near the tar pits and Fire Plume Ridge are becoming a serious hazard. Their migration paths now cross every supply route in the crater's interior.

Kill eight of the large herbivores and predators combined — stegodons, stompers, and thunderers. Give the rest of the herd reason to move.

the_devilsaur.name: The Devilsaur
the_devilsaur.description:
There is a devilsaur that roams the eastern reaches of Un'Goro. It has destroyed two scouting parties and blocked passage through the crater's eastern corridor for weeks.

Apex predators this dangerous can only be handled one way. Draw it out — cut down the silithid ravagers it competes with for territory and it will come to investigate. Return with its tooth.

objectives.description: Kill 6 Silithid Ravagers to draw the Devilsaur

slithering_scar_queen_quest.name: The Slithering Scar Queen
slithering_scar_queen_quest.description:
The silithid colony at the Slithering Scar is being directed by a queen unlike any we have observed. She has already ordered three expansion surges and absorbed two rival insect colonies.

Fight into the hive. Kill the ravager guards at the tunnel entrance to force her out of her burrow, then bring back a plate of her carapace.

objectives.description: Kill 8 Silithid Ravagers at the hive entrance

encrypted_twilight_texts.name: Encrypted Twilight Texts
encrypted_twilight_texts.description:
The Twilight's Hammer cult encrypts its orders and scripture in fragments carried by every member. Each scrap alone is meaningless, but ten together begin to reveal the shape of their plan.

Kill the cultists near Southwind ruins — marauders, stonecallers, avengers, whoever you find — and bring me ten text fragments. The cult doesn't know we can read their cipher.

wings_of_the_hive.name: Wings of the Hive
wings_of_the_hive.description:
Hive Zora's wasp caste has been scouting further and further from the nest. Two Cenarion outriders were intercepted last week before they could reach the Hold.

Cull the wasp population. Bring back eight Hive'Zora wings from the wasps and reavers patrolling the hive perimeter. Fewer wings means fewer scouts.

venom_of_hive_regal.name: Venom of Hive Regal
venom_of_hive_regal.description:
The venom produced by Hive'Regal stingers is unlike anything our alchemists have encountered — it breaks down magical barriers as readily as flesh. We need samples for study before it's used against us.

Kill eight Hive'Regal stingers and overseers and collect eight venom sacs. Handle them with the kit I'll give you — bare hands will not suffice.

essence_of_wind.name: Essence of Wind
essence_of_wind.description:
The wind elementals roaming Silithus carry a compressed essence of elemental power that our enchanters can use to reinforce our protective wards around Cenarion Hold.

Bring me six wind essences extracted from dust stormers and whirling sandstorms. The extraction vials are fragile, so work carefully.

obsidian_shards.name: Obsidian Shards
obsidian_shards.description:
The Obsidian Destroyers that wander the Abyssal Sands are a new emergence — we didn't see them in this region six months ago. Something is awakening the deeper rock elementals.

Kill five obsidian destroyers and collect their cores before the material disperses. I want to map where they're forming.

sand_terror_bounty.name: Sand Terror Bounty
sand_terror_bounty.description:
The Sand Terrors have claimed two excavation sites in the past month, ambushing from beneath the ground without warning. We're offering a bounty on their claws as proof of a kill.

Bring me six sand terror claws. Each claw is distinctive — no substitutes. The bounty is good until the sites are clear.

assault_on_hive_zora.name: Assault on Hive Zora
assault_on_hive_zora.description:
Hive Zora is the most active colony in Silithus right now. Their reavers have been ranging out in coordinated hunting parties — something the silithid don't do unless the colony is preparing to surge.

Hit them hard before the surge begins. Kill ten reavers and six hive guards inside the colony tunnels. Break their momentum.

into_hive_regal.name: Into Hive Regal
into_hive_regal.description:
Hive Regal has begun producing a new caste — the colossi. We've never seen this before. If they complete the caste development and start fielding colossi in numbers, they will break through our perimeter.

Push into Hive Regal. Kill four colossi before the caste matures, and six of the flyers screening the tunnels.

the_high_marshal.name: The High Marshal
the_high_marshal.description:
The Twilight High Marshal has been coordinating every cult operation in Silithus for years — the windstone rituals, the encrypted orders, the alliances with the silithid. He does not leave the Crystal Vale himself.

Force his hand. Cut down his avengers until he has no choice but to face you personally. Return with his insignia.

ancient_sand_reaver_quest.name: Ancient Sand Reaver
ancient_sand_reaver_quest.description:
There is something in the Abyssal Sands that we have no record of. Our seismic surveys show a body mass that dwarfs any sand terror we have encountered — something ancient, and it has been moving closer to Cenarion Hold each month.

Hunt the Sand Terrors in its territory to draw it out. Whatever emerges, kill it and bring back a scale as proof.

objectives.description: Kill 6 Sand Terrors to draw the Ancient Reaver
